question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing the Consumer Price Index increased exactly 0.3% for the month. Since 0.3% is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The current market price of 68% appears to reflect outdated information or market participants who haven't yet processed the official BLS release.
key_factors: Official BLS release confirms March 2026 CPI increased 0.3%
0.3% is well below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution
Market resolution criteria are objective and based on official government data
No possibility of revision large enough to change outcome
risks: Potential data revision by BLS (historically very small for monthly CPI)
Market participants may be slow to update based on official release
Possible confusion about seasonally vs non-seasonally adjusted figures
